descriptions: The Syndicate is listed as an ally of Rockers MC in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Hells Angels MC Death Riders MC , Evil Ones MC , The Syndicate , Redliners , Crack Down Posse

descriptions: Infobox lists Azande Ani Kpi Gbe as an ally of Wagner Group.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: List: ... Azande Ani Kpi Gbe

400 Mawozo.description: The 400 Mawozo is the largest gang in Haiti, based in Ganthier and the Port-au-Prince suburbs of Tabarre and Pétion-Ville. Founded around 2020, it consists largely of deportees, former smugglers, and police officers. The gang is known for kidnappings, murder, and rape, and gained international attention in 2021 for kidnapping U.S. missionaries. Its leader is Wilson Joseph, also known as Lanmò Sanjou.

descriptions: 400 Mawozo and Chen Mechan clashed in 2022.
edges.evidence_quote: In April–May 2022, clashes between the rival gangs, 400 Mawozo and Chen Mechan, occurred in the Plain of the Cul-de-Sac area.

edges.target: Revolutionary Forces of the G9 Family and Allies
descriptions: 400 Mawozo lists Revolutionary Forces of the G9 Family and Allies as rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Revolutionary Forces of the G9 Family and Allies

descriptions: 400 Mawozo lists Zoe Pound as rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Zoe Pound

edges.source: Revolutionary Forces of the G9 Family and Allies
descriptions: 400 Mawozo is listed as a rival of G9 in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: 400 Mawozo

The Westies.description: The Westies were an Irish criminal gang based in Blanchardstown, west Dublin, Ireland. They controlled the heroin trade in west Dublin during the late 1990s and early 2000s, and were known for extreme violence against drug addicts and rival dealers. The gang also engaged in armed robberies and extortion. The organization imploded after the murders of all its leaders.

descriptions: Sons of Silence obtained a mobile drug lab from the wife of Barhoppers president.
edges.evidence_quote: a mobile drug lab obtained from Cecelia Martinez – wife of the imprisoned Barhoppers Motorcycle Club president Glen Gary

descriptions: KIA is the armed wing of KIO.
edges.evidence_quote: the Kachin Independence Army (KIA), under the Kachin Independence Organisation (KIO)

descriptions: KIA and TNLA are both members of the Northern Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: the Northern Alliance—which consists of four insurgent groups, the Arakan Army (AA), the Kachin Independence Army (KIA), the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA) and the Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA)

descriptions: TNLA received public support from KIA.
edges.evidence_quote: the TNLA received public support from ... other rebel groups like the KIA

descriptions: Franco-Murina is a splinter of Tegano.
edges.evidence_quote: Operazione TNT 2 , inizio 2015: otto arresti nella cosca Franco-Murina, costola dei Tegano, per associazione a delinquere di stampo mafioso , traffico di esplosivo e droga .

Annihilators Motorcycle Club.description: Canadian outlaw biker club and organized crime group active in the 1980s and 1990s. Based in Chatham, Ontario, it was founded in 1988 by Wayne Kellestine as a puppet club for the Outlaws MC. Engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, armed robbery, murder, assault, and kidnapping. Disbanded in 1999.

descriptions: The Annihilators considered the Hells Angels as rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hells Angels

descriptions: The Annihilators merged into the Loners in 1999.
edges.evidence_quote: On June 2, 1999, the Annihilators Motorcycle Club, joined the larger Loners Motorcycle Club based in Woodbridge.

descriptions: Annihilators MC patched over to become part of Loners MC.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1999 the Annihilators would 'patch-over' to the Loners Motorcycle Club

descriptions: The Annihilators were a support club for the Outlaws until the late 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Outlaws MC

descriptions: The Holocaust club was a puppet club of the Outlaws.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1982, the Outlaw chapter in London, Ontario arranged to have the Amherstburg Queensmen move up to the London area to serve as a puppet club, which was renamed as the Holocaust Motorcycle Club

descriptions: Infobox lists Annihilators MC as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Annihilators MC

descriptions: The Loners absorbed the Annihilators Motorcycle Club in 1999.
edges.evidence_quote: In June 1999, had established its Chatham chapter (based near St. Thomas), when they absorbed the Annihilators Motorcycle Club led by Wayne Kellestine and Giovanni Muscedere.

descriptions: The Queensmen were renamed to the Holocaust club.
edges.evidence_quote: The club began as the Queensmen Motorcycle Club based in Amherstburg. ... renamed as the Holocaust Motorcycle Club

descriptions: The Tajik government claims the Islamic Renaissance Party of Tajikistan is allied with ISIS–K, but the party denies it.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Islamic Renaissance Party of Tajikistan (claimed by Tajik government; denied)

'Ndrina Mancuso.description: The 'Ndrina Mancuso is a powerful 'Ndrangheta clan based in Limbadi and Nicotera, in the province of Vibo Valentia, Calabria. Considered the most influential clan in the province, it is involved in international cocaine trafficking, extortion, usury, public procurement fraud, and money laundering. The clan has alliances with other 'Ndrangheta families, Colombian cartels, and the FARC, and operates in northern Italy, central Italy, and abroad, notably in Togo. It has been described as the financially most powerful criminal clan in Europe.

descriptions: Accorinti planned retaliation against Mancuso after a murder and injury in 2003.
edges.evidence_quote: A partire dal 2003 con l'omicidio di Raffaele Fiammingo, vicino agli Accorinti e il ferimento di Francesco Mancuso, alias Tabacco , gli Accorinti stavano preparando a Roma una ritorsione contro Michele Mancuso

descriptions: Accorinti has ties with Mancuso 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: Gli accorinti hanno legami con ... i Mancuso

descriptions: Mancuso, along with Pesce, Piromalli, and Bellocco, mediated peace for the Albanese in the Laureana di Borrello feud.
edges.evidence_quote: si risolve con una pace voluta dai Mancuso - Pesce - Piromalli e Bellocco

descriptions: Anello and Mancuso were targeted together in Operation Rubamazzo.

descriptions: The Barbaro-Papalia, Mancuso, and Ursino-Macrì were involved in drug trafficking together.

descriptions: The Looney gang and William Gabel's gang were at war.
edges.evidence_quote: The Looney gang entered into a war with William Gabel's gang.

'Ndrina Lamalfa.description: The 'Ndrina Lamalfa is a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in San Ferdinando, Italy. It was involved in the kidnapping and murder of Michele Arcangelo Tripodi in 1990, whose body was found in 1997. The clan has been active since at least the 1990s and continues to operate.

Norman Conks.description: Large Catholic sectarian street gang active in Glasgow, based in the Norman Street area of Bridgeton. Active from the 1880s to the 1960s, they evolved from a penny mob into an influential gang. Known for street fighting with the Protestant Billy Boys and friendly relations with the Catholic Calton Tongs. Included young women members.

descriptions: The Billy Boys and Norman Conks frequently clashed due to sectarian and territorial rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: the Protestant Bridgeton (or Billy) Boys who frequently clashed with the Roman Catholic Norman Conks in the East End of the city

descriptions: The Norman Conks and Billy Boys were engaged in street fighting, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: They were often involved in street fighting with the Protestant Billy Boys of Bridgeton

descriptions: The Norman Conks had friendly relations with the Calton Tongs, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: but were on friendlier terms with the Calton Tongs who were also mostly Catholic

descriptions: The Norman Conks were formerly known as Penny Mobs.
edges.evidence_quote: two of the most infamous were the former Penny Mobs , the Bridgeton Billy Boys and the Norman Conks

Gooch Close Gang.description: Organised crime group based in Moss Side, South Manchester, England, founded in the late 1980s. Primarily involved in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, robbery, and murder. Known for violent disputes with rival gangs such as the Doddington Gang and Moss Side Bloods. Still active as of the present.

descriptions: C Block claims affiliation with Gooch Close Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: also claims affiliation to Gooch and Crips through family ties and friendships

descriptions: Gooch Close Gang bought and sold drugs to Cheetham Hill, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Later, The Gooch Close Gang often bought and sold drugs to Cheetham Hill. The Gooch had close ties to Cheetham Hill, with the cousin of the Cheetham Hill Gang's head being a leading figure in the Gooch.

descriptions: Doddington gang was rival of Gooch Close Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang became rivals of the Cheetham Hill Gang and the Gooch Close Gang during the late 1980s and early 1990s

descriptions: Doddington gang had a fierce rivalry with Gooch Close Gang over drug trade.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1990s, the Pepperhill/Doddington group developed a fierce rivalry with the Gooch Close Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: The other FMD members were subsequently drawn into gang culture, allying themselves with the Gooch

descriptions: Masseria family and Castellammarese clan fought each other in the Castellammarese War.

Tiny Rascal Gang.description: Primarily Cambodian-American street gang based in Long Beach, California, founded in 1981 by Cambodian youth refugees for protection. Engages in drug trafficking, murder, robbery, and weapons trafficking. Has an estimated 5,000-10,000 members and associates as of 2009. Active in the United States and Canada.

39ers gang.description: Hybrid street gang formed in 2010 from the merger of the G-Strip gang (Gallier Street) and 3-N-G gang (Third and Galvez) in New Orleans. Primarily African American, involved in drug trafficking, murder, and racketeering. Linked to over 45 murders, including the 2010 killing of rapper Magnolia Shorty. Ten members were indicted and convicted in 2017.

Crips.description: The Crips are a primarily African-American alliance of street gangs based in Southern California, founded in Los Angeles in 1969 by Raymond Washington and Stanley Williams. They are one of the largest and most violent street gang associations in the United States, with an estimated 30,000 to 35,000 members in 2008. Members are involved in murders, robberies, and drug dealing, and have a long-standing rivalry with the Bloods.
